user.title: EV Charger Load Balancing
user.description: Configure the power meter and electrical service parameters. This integration supports multiple EV chargers per power meter with configurable priorities.

data.unavailable_fallback_current: Fallback current (for 'Set a specific current' mode)

data_description.power_meter_entity: Sensor entity that reports total service power in Watts.
data_description.voltage: Nominal supply voltage in Volts (used to convert W ↔ A).
data_description.max_service_current: Service current limit in Amps. Charging will never exceed this limit. You can set this lower than your actual breaker rating to reserve a safety margin — for example, enter 28 A on a 32 A service to keep 4 A free for other loads. It can also represent a subcircuit or any custom power budget.
data_description.action_stop_charging: Script entity called to stop charging when headroom is insufficient. Receives a 'charger_id' variable.
data_description.action_start_charging: Script entity called to start/resume charging after it was stopped. Receives a 'charger_id' variable.
data_description.charger_status_entity: Sensor whose state equals 'Charging' when the EV is actively charging (e.g. from the OCPP integration). When set, the balancer uses this to avoid over-subtracting headroom while the charger is idle or finished.

error.entity_not_found: The selected power meter entity was not found.

abort.already_configured: EV Charger Load Balancing is already configured.

init.title: EV Charger Load Balancing Settings
init.description: Update the global settings. The power meter sensor cannot be changed after initial setup. After saving, you will configure each charger's action scripts, status sensor, and priority.

data_description.voltage: Nominal supply voltage in Volts (used to convert W ↔ A).
data_description.max_service_current: Service current limit in Amps. Charging will never exceed this limit. You can set this lower than your actual breaker rating to reserve a safety margin — for example, enter 28 A on a 32 A service to keep 4 A free for other loads. It can also represent a subcircuit or any custom power budget.
data_description.unavailable_behavior: What to do when the power meter becomes unavailable: stop all chargers (safest) or use each charger's individual fallback current.
data_description.unavailable_fallback_current: Legacy: charging current (A) used when the unavailable-behavior mode is 'Set a specific current' (an older setting no longer shown in the dropdown). This has been superseded by the per-charger fallback current configured on each charger step. Preserved here so existing entries can still be adjusted.

charger.title: Configure Charger
charger.description: Configure action scripts, status sensor, and priority for charger {charger_num}. Priority controls how available current is distributed when multiple chargers compete for headroom.

data_description.action_set_current: Script called to set the charging current. Receives variables: current_a (amps), current_w (watts), charger_id (config entry ID), and charger_num (1-based charger index).
data_description.action_stop_charging: Script called when the balancer decides this charger should stop charging. Receives variables: charger_id (config entry ID) and charger_num (1-based charger index).
data_description.action_start_charging: Script called when the balancer decides this charger should start or resume charging. Receives variables: charger_id (config entry ID) and charger_num (1-based charger index).
data_description.charger_status_entity: Sensor whose state is exactly 'Charging' when this charger is actively charging. Used to avoid over-subtracting headroom while the charger is idle or finished.
data_description.priority: Priority weight for this charger (0–100, step 5). Higher values receive proportionally more available current. Equal priorities result in equal distribution.
data_description.charger_fallback_current: Charging current (A) applied to this charger when the power meter is unavailable and the fallback mode is 'Use per-charger fallback current'. Capped at the max charger current.
data_description.add_another_charger: Enable to configure another charger with its own priority.

error.duplicate_charger_status: This status sensor is already used by another charger (in this or another load balancer instance). Please select a different sensor.
